text: NetworkRepository (NR) – A Dynamic Data Sharing Repository for Graph Analysis NetworkRepository (NR) is an interactive data repository that links scalable storage with powerful data analysis and visualization tools. It is the first data repository designed to provide all the services needed to enable data sharing and collaborative data analysis. While data repositories such as the UCI Machine Learning Repository or SNAP provide an archive of data for download, they do not give the user any “remote sensing” abilities to examine the data in a more interactive fashion. Similarly, they do not help facilitate data understanding or comparison of different data sets along multiple dimensions. NR aims at reducing the scientist data search and understanding time and thus enables fast data exploration. In addition to data understanding, one major distinction of NR is that it provides a large number of large scale graph datasets and more interactive graph visualization capabilities compared to current data repositories. In contrast to most data repositories that do not include social features that allows collaboration and sharing of knowledge among researchers, NR enables users to comment on specific data sets, to “like” observations, and to share visualizations, thus providing a dynamic medium to advance science.
